title: Delicate Roasted Ethiopian Teff Injera
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish with a twist, featuring roasted teff injera with a cheese pull, made with red food coloring and long-fermentation dough, perfect for meal prep and FODMAP friendly

ingredients.name: teff flour
ingredients.notes: make sure to use gluten-free teff flour

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine teff flour, water, yeast, and salt, and mix well to form a smooth batter.
Add the red food coloring and mix until the color is evenly distributed.
Cover the bowl with a cloth and let the mixture ferment in a warm place for 24 hours.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Pour the fermented batter into a 9x13 inch baking dish and smooth out the top.
Drizzle the olive oil over the batter and sprinkle the shredded mozzarella cheese on top.
Roast the injera in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the cheese is melted and pulled.
Remove the injera from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
